findings.title: Integer overflow before ExAllocatePoolWithTag at 0x1402DD436
findings.description: Arithmetic (imul/shl/mul) at 0x1402DD422 precedes ExAllocatePoolWithTag call at 0x1402DD436 with no overflow guard (no jo/jb between them). If the multiplied value is user-controlled, this causes a heap overflow.

findings.title: DKOM: EPROCESS walk + token steal pattern (Win10_1507 offsets)
findings.description: Driver accesses EPROCESS offsets for ActiveProcessLinks (0x2f0) and Token (0x358), matching Win10_1507 layout. This is the classic token-stealing privilege escalation pattern: walk the EPROCESS linked list, find System process, copy its token to the target.

findings.title: Double-fetch (TOCTOU) at 0x140027499
findings.description: Two reads from [reg+0x0] at 0x14002742C and 0x140027499 with a cmp/test using the first value and the second value flowing into a sensitive sink (allocator / memcpy / deref). An attacker can race to change the buffer between check and use.

findings.title: Missing mitigation: Signature enforcement (FORCE_INTEGRITY)
findings.description: Signature enforcement (FORCE_INTEGRITY) is not enabled. Driver can load without a valid Authenticode signature.

findings.title: Driver is NOT HVCI-compatible
findings.description: Driver fails HVCI checks (missing FORCE_INTEGRITY or has W+X sections). Cannot load in HVCI-enabled environments without policy bypass.

findings.title: Kernel write gadgets found (8 instances)
findings.description: Multiple 'mov [reg+offset], reg' patterns in driver code. If the register is derived from user input (e.g., IOCTL buffer), this is an arbitrary kernel memory write primitive.

details.gadgets: 0x1400013E1: mov dword ptr [rsp + 0x40], ebx; 0x14000140E: mov dword ptr [rsp + 0x48], eax; 0x140001436: mov dword ptr [rsp + 0x38], eax; 0x140001458: mov dword ptr [rsp + 0x38], eax

findings.title: Hidden function with purpose 'runtime resolve' at 0x1402DC5E1
findings.description: Function at 0x1402DC5E1 performs 'runtime resolve' but is not reachable via the normal DriverEntry → MajorFunction call graph.  It may be invoked via a timer, DPC, work item, or kernel callback — a hidden secondary attack path.

findings.title: No PreviousMode / RequestorMode check detected
findings.description: Driver does not import ExGetPreviousMode or KeGetPreviousMode and no inline KTHREAD.PreviousMode read was found. Without checking PreviousMode, a kernel-mode caller could be impersonated, or user-mode requests may bypass validation. IOCTLs should verify RequestorMode == UserMode before trusting input.

findings.title: IOCTL handlers missing InputBufferLength validation
findings.description: The following IOCTL handlers do not appear to validate InputBufferLength before processing the buffer: 0x49493032, 0x49656E69, 0x000A089C, 0x000A089D. Without size checks, a short buffer could cause pool overread or out-of-bounds writes.

findings.title: IOCTL handlers missing NULL buffer check
findings.description: These IOCTL handlers may not check for NULL SystemBuffer before dereferencing: 0x46493031, 0x49493031, 0x49493032. A zero-length IOCTL request can result in SystemBuffer=NULL, causing a kernel NULL-pointer dereference (BSOD).

findings.title: No privilege check APIs imported
findings.description: Driver handles IOCTLs but does not import SeSinglePrivilegeCheck, SePrivilegeCheck, or SeAccessCheck. Sensitive operations should verify caller privileges beyond just device handle access rights.

findings.title: Driver has Authenticode signature
findings.description: PE contains a WIN_CERTIFICATE structure with embedded signature.

findings.title: Signed by: WDKTestCert DESKTOP-EMPB1EL$,134073963055245640
findings.description:
Subject: WDKTestCert DESKTOP-EMPB1EL$,134073963055245640 ()
Issuer: WDKTestCert DESKTOP-EMPB1EL$,134073963055245640
Serial: 7a2e50c3bd5329a44d00da02a181cf2b
Key: RSA 2048-bit
Valid until: 2035-11-12T00:00:00+00:00

findings.title: Driver signed with SELF-SIGNED certificate
findings.description: The signing certificate is self-signed (issuer == subject). Self-signed drivers have no trust chain and cannot be verified by the OS certificate store. This requires test-signing mode to be enabled, which is a significant security downgrade. Malware often uses self-signed certs.

findings.title: No timestamp countersignature detected
findings.description: The Authenticode signature has no countersignature timestamp. Without a timestamp, the signature becomes invalid after the signing certificate expires. Timestamped signatures remain valid indefinitely. This affects driver loadability.

findings.title: PKCS#7 signature verifies cryptographically
findings.description: The primary Authenticode PKCS#7 signature was verified against the signer certificate's public key.

findings.title: Authenticode PE hash matches (binary not tampered)
findings.description: Recomputed sha256 hash of the PE image matches the value embedded in SpcIndirectDataContent.

findings.title: Nested signature(s) present: sha256
findings.description: The primary signature is dual-signed — a second (typically SHA-256) signature is nested via MsSpcNestedSignature. Windows on modern Windows 10+ / HVCI prefers the SHA-256 side for verification.

findings.title: Chain does not terminate at a Windows kernel-trusted root
findings.description: The certificate chain does not chain up to any root present in the Windows kernel trust store (20 known roots checked by thumbprint). Unless a missing intermediate can be supplied by the OS at load time, Windows will not accept this driver.

findings.title: Load restricted: Self-signed certificate
findings.description: This driver is self-signed. It will only load on Windows with test-signing mode enabled (bcdedit /set testsigning on). A watermark appears on the desktop when test-signing is active.

findings.title: HVCI incompatible: missing FORCE_INTEGRITY flag
findings.description: This driver is NOT compatible with Hypervisor-protected Code Integrity (HVCI / Memory Integrity). On systems with HVCI enabled, Windows will BLOCK this driver from loading. Issues: missing FORCE_INTEGRITY flag.

findings.title: Driver architecture: x64
findings.description: 64-bit driver — loads on x64 Windows

findings.title: Load compatibility: BLOCKED — 4 issue(s) prevent loading
findings.description:
[BLOCKED] Kernel Trust Anchor: Chain does not terminate at a Windows kernel trust root (no-ms-root)
  [BLOCKED] Self-Signed: Self-signed drivers require test-signing mode
  [BLOCKED] HVCI: Blocked when Memory Integrity is ON (missing FORCE_INTEGRITY flag)
  [BLOCKED] Secure Boot: Blocked when Secure Boot is enabled

blockers: FORCE_INTEGRITY flag not set (HVCI requires it on the PE header)

passes: Any valid signature accepted under test-signing

blockers: No WHQL attestation EKU — S Mode / Secured-Core require WHQL-signed drivers

findings.title: ROP/JOP gadgets found: 12205 total (misc: 5611, reg-control: 2789, memory-write: 1752, memory-read: 1501, stack-pivot: 481, jmp-reg: 63, syscall: 8)
findings.description:
Found 12205 usable ROP/JOP gadgets in executable sections. Since many drivers lack ASLR, these gadgets are at predictable addresses and can be chained for kernel exploitation.

Top gadgets by type:
  [stack-pivot] (481 found):
    0x140001527: pop rsp ; pop r13 ; pop r14 ; pop r15 ; ret
    0x14000173C: xchg ecx, eax ; add byte ptr [rax], al ; xor eax, eax ; add rsp, 0x28 ; ret
    0x1400031FE: leave ; jg 0x14000320b ; mov eax, 0xfffffffa ; add rsp, 0x28 ; ret
  [memory-write] (1752 found):
    0x14000136B: mov dword ptr [rip + 0x2cff47], 1 ; xor eax, eax ; ret
    0x140001369: jmp 0x140001375 ; mov dword ptr [rip + 0x2cff47], 1 ; xor eax, eax ; ret
    0x140001367: add byte ptr [rax], al ; jmp 0x140001375 ; mov dword ptr [rip + 0x2cff47], 1 ; xor eax, eax ; ret
  [memory-read] (1501 found):
    0x140001310: mov gs, word ptr [rdi - 0x3f7affed] ; je 0x14000131f ; xor eax, eax ; add rsp, 0x28 ; ret
    0x14000130E: adc al, ch ; mov gs, word ptr [rdi - 0x3f7affed] ; je 0x14000131f ; xor eax, eax ; add rsp, 0x28 ; ret
    0x14000130A: add byte ptr [rbp - 0x17ef8b40], al ; mov gs, word ptr [rdi - 0x3f7affed] ; je 0x14000131f ; xor eax, eax ; add rsp, 0x28 ; ret
  [reg-control] (2789 found):
    0x14000152D: pop rdi ; ret
    0x14000152C: pop r15 ; ret
    0x14000152B: pop rsi ; pop r15 ; ret
  [jmp-reg] (63 found):
    0x1400012D4: adc al, 0 ; jmp rcx
    0x1400012D3: xchg ebp, eax ; adc al, 0 ; jmp rcx
    0x1400012D1: ret 0x9520 ; adc al, 0 ; jmp rcx
